page: Microsoft Azure GS1 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the GS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor. This processor provides 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 40 MB L3 cache, 32 KB L1d cache, and 0.25 MB L2 cache, with support for AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instruction sets.

The instance is equipped with 28.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 14.0 GB of RAM per core. It also includes 57 GB of local storage. No GPU accelerators are present on this server type, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates that the GS1 exhibits limited processing and memory throughput capabilities. In stress-ng CPU tests, both single-core and multi-core performance fall into the bottom 10% tier. Memory bandwidth tests show weak performance for small cached blocks and poor performance for large uncached blocks, with read speeds at 20.49 GB/sec and write speeds at 13.0 GB/sec. Similarly, database operations and static web serving throughput are positioned in the bottom 10% of benchmarks. However, single-threaded gzip compression achieves an average tier result at 23.85 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the GS1 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high compute performance. Due to its low CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results, it is best suited for legacy database hosting, basic caching layers, or lightweight memory-bound applications rather than high-throughput web serving or intensive data processing.
description: Microsoft Azure GS1 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz. It features 28.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 14.0 GB per core, alongside 57 GB of local storage. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance lacks GPU acceleration. Benchmark results place its CPU and memory bandwidth performance largely in the lower tiers, with stress-ng and Passmark scores falling in the bottom 10%, though single-threaded gzip compression performs at an average level. Given its high memory capacity relative to compute resources, the GS1 is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads. It is suited for lightweight database hosting, basic caching, and memory-bound legacy applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure GS1 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine configured with 2 vCPUs, 28.0 GB of RAM, and 57 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive legacy workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for low-compute, memory-bound workloads.

28.0 GB of system memory providing 14.0 GB per core
Suited for lightweight database hosting and basic caching layers
